description: Curated public video/speech sources for the Steve Jobs brain. All are freely available public appearances, interviews, keynotes, and speeches — no copyrighted book content.

source_types.speech: Commencement addresses, internal talks, public remarks
source_types.internal: Internal Apple meetings captured on video
source_types.documentary: Clips from documentaries with Jobs speaking directly

sources.title: Think Different internal meeting (1997)
sources.description: Jobs explains Apple's brand strategy after returning. 'Marketing is about values.' Often called the greatest marketing video of all time.
sources.youtube_search: Steve Jobs Think Different internal meeting 1997

sources.title: Stanford commencement speech (2005)
sources.description: Three stories: connecting the dots, love and loss, death. 'Stay hungry, stay foolish.' His most personal public statement.
sources.youtube_search: Steve Jobs Stanford commencement speech 2005 full

sources.title: The Lost Interview (1995)
sources.description: 70-minute interview filmed during NeXT years, lost and rediscovered. Candid views on product, Microsoft, creativity, management.
sources.youtube_search: Steve Jobs The Lost Interview 1995 full

sources.title: D Conference with Walt Mossberg (2003)
sources.description: Wide-ranging conversation at D: All Things Digital. iTunes, music industry, Apple strategy.
sources.youtube_search: Steve Jobs D Conference Walt Mossberg 2003

sources.title: D Conference with Walt Mossberg (2005)
sources.description: Follow-up D interview. iPod ecosystem, Intel switch, vision for digital hub.
sources.youtube_search: Steve Jobs D Conference Walt Mossberg 2005

sources.title: D8 Conference with Walt Mossberg (2010)
sources.description: Jobs' last major D interview. Thoughts on Flash, iPad, Google, privacy, and the post-PC era.

sources.title: iPhone introduction keynote (2007)
sources.description: Macworld 2007. 'An iPod, a phone, and an internet communicator.' The defining product launch of the century.
sources.youtube_search: Steve Jobs iPhone introduction 2007 full keynote

sources.title: Macintosh introduction (1984)
sources.description: The original Mac launch. '1984 won't be like 1984.' Jobs at 28, pure showmanship.

sources.title: iPad introduction keynote (2010)
sources.description: Jobs introduces the iPad — a product category many mocked. Turned out to be a billion-dollar business.

sources.title: WWDC 1997 — Responding to insult
sources.description: An audience member insults Jobs. His response about starting with the customer experience is legendary.
sources.youtube_search: Steve Jobs WWDC 1997 insult response customer experience

sources.title: NeXT introduction (1988)
sources.description: Jobs launches NeXT Computer. Over-engineered and over-priced, but the OS became macOS. Beautiful failure that became a foundation.

sources.title: Macworld 1998 — iMac introduction
sources.description: The Bondi Blue iMac. Design as strategy. Jobs proves that aesthetics move hardware.

sources.title: iPod introduction (2001)
sources.description: '1,000 songs in your pocket.' The product that funded the iPhone. Scroll wheel as interaction design.

sources.title: Apple WWDC 2011 — iCloud introduction
sources.description: Jobs' last major public appearance. Introduces iCloud and the post-PC vision. Visibly ill but still commanding.

sources.title: Smithsonian oral history interview (1995)
sources.description: Detailed interview about his career, Apple's founding, Woz, design philosophy. Scholarly but personal.

sources.title: Playboy interview (1985)
sources.description: Long-form interview right before getting fired. Candid about vision, ego, and where computing is going.

sources.title: ABC Nightline interview (1999)
sources.description: Short but revealing interview about Apple's comeback, the importance of taste, and why most products suck.

sources.title: Gates & Jobs together at D5 (2007)
sources.description: Historic joint interview with Bill Gates. Mutual respect, philosophical differences, views on the future.
sources.youtube_search: Steve Jobs Bill Gates D5 conference 2007 full

sources.title: Thoughts on Flash — Apple.com (2010)
sources.description: Published open letter. Jobs' clearest articulation of platform strategy and saying no to incumbents.

sources.title: Internal talk — 'Bicycle for the mind'
sources.description: Jobs explains the computer as a bicycle for the mind — amplifying human capability rather than replacing it.
sources.youtube_search: Steve Jobs bicycle for the mind
